questions.question_text: How many total hours of pre-license education does Missouri require for a salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Missouri requires 72 total hours of pre-license education for salesperson candidates: a 48-hour pre-exam course (taken first) plus a 24-hour Missouri Real Estate Practice (MREP) course. Both courses must be completed through MREC-approved education providers.

questions.question_text: A salesperson candidate completes the 24-hour MREP course first, then enrolls in the 48-hour pre-exam course. Is this sequence acceptable?
questions.correct_answer: No, the 48-hour course must be first
distractors: No, the courses must be taken together
questions.explanation: Missouri requires the 48-hour pre-exam course to be completed before the 24-hour MREP course. Taking them out of order does not satisfy the licensing requirements, even if both courses are completed through MREC-approved providers. The 48-hour course provides foundational knowledge needed for the MREP course.

questions.question_text: What does MREP stand for in Missouri real estate education?
questions.explanation: MREP stands for Missouri Real Estate Practice. This 24-hour course is the second required pre-license course and focuses on practical skills licensees will need. It must be completed after the 48-hour pre-exam course and before applying for licensure.

questions.question_text: A candidate finds an online real estate course not listed on the MREC website. The school claims state approval is pending. Can the candidate use this course toward licensure?
distractors: Yes, if the school is accredited
Yes, if the course covers required topics
Yes, with provisional credit pending approval
questions.explanation: Missouri requires all pre-license education to be completed through MREC-approved providers. A course with pending approval has not yet been approved, and completing it provides no credit toward licensure. Candidates should verify provider approval on the MREC website before enrolling.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed 48 hours of pre-license education in Kansas. She moves to Missouri and wants credit applied. What is the most likely result?
distractors: Full credit transfers automatically to MO
MREC grants partial credit of 24 hours
She only needs the 24-hour MREP course
questions.explanation: Missouri requires pre-license education through MREC-approved providers and courses. Out-of-state education generally does not transfer as credit toward Missouri pre-license requirements. The candidate will need to complete the full 72 hours through Missouri-approved providers.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to apply for a Missouri real estate salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Missouri requires salesperson license applicants to be at least 18 years of age. This is consistent with the age of majority in Missouri and the ability to enter into binding contracts, which is essential for real estate practice.

questions.question_text: Which testing vendor administers the Missouri real estate licensing examination?
questions.explanation: PSI Services LLC is the testing vendor contracted to administer the Missouri real estate licensing examination. Candidates must schedule their exam through PSI after completing their education requirements.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the 48-hour pre-exam course in January. By what month must she apply for her license to avoid retaking the course?
distractors: There is no time limit to apply
questions.explanation: Missouri requires candidates to apply for licensure within 6 months of completing the 48-hour pre-exam course. After 6 months, the course credit expires and the candidate must retake it. This ensures that new licensees have current knowledge when entering the profession.

questions.question_text: Which requirement is part of the Missouri salesperson license application process?
questions.explanation: Missouri requires fingerprinting as part of the license application to facilitate a criminal background check. This helps MREC evaluate whether an applicant has a disqualifying criminal history. Financial statements, E&O insurance, and health exams are not application requirements.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed the 48-hour course 7 months ago and just passed the state exam. MREC will most likely take which action?
distractors: Issue the license with a probation period
Grant the license since the exam was passed
Require only the MREP course again
questions.explanation: Missouri requires applicants to apply within 6 months of completing the 48-hour pre-exam course. At 7 months, the application deadline has passed. Even though the candidate passed the exam, MREC will not issue the license; the candidate must re-establish eligibility, which requires completing the 48-hour course again (and, because the course must be completed on or before the exam date, may require retesting).

questions.question_text: How many months of active salesperson experience does Missouri require before a person can apply for a broker license?
questions.explanation: Missouri requires broker applicants to have been an active salesperson for at least 24 of the previous 30 months. This ensures broker candidates have substantial recent practical experience before taking on the responsibilities of operating independently and supervising other licensees.

questions.question_text: A salesperson has been licensed for 26 months but was on inactive status for 8 of those months. Does she meet the broker experience requirement?
questions.correct_answer: No, she has only 18 active months
distractors: Yes, inactive months count at half value
No, she must wait a full 30 months
questions.explanation: The broker requirement is 24 months of active salesperson experience within the last 30 months. Inactive months do not count. With only 18 active months (26 minus 8), she falls short of the 24-month active requirement.

questions.question_text: What additional education must a salesperson complete to qualify for a Missouri broker license?
questions.explanation: Missouri requires broker applicants to complete a 48-hour broker pre-exam course in addition to meeting the experience requirement of 24 active months within the last 30 months. This course covers advanced topics including brokerage management and supervision.

questions.question_text: A salesperson from Kansas with 36 months of active experience wants a Missouri broker license. What must she demonstrate?
distractors: She must first get a Missouri SP license
She needs 48 months out of state
questions.explanation: Missouri accepts out-of-state salesperson experience: an applicant holding a current, active salesperson license in another jurisdiction who has 24 of the last 30 months of active salesperson experience may qualify. She must provide a certified license history, complete the 48-hour Missouri broker pre-exam course, and pass both portions of the broker exam.

questions.question_text: Which exam must a broker candidate pass in addition to completing the broker pre-exam course?
distractors: Only a state law portion retake
No exam if experience is sufficient
questions.explanation: Missouri broker candidates must pass a separate broker licensing examination after completing the 48-hour broker pre-exam course and meeting the experience requirement. The broker exam tests additional content beyond what the salesperson exam covers, including brokerage management and supervision topics.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must a Missouri licensee complete per renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Missouri licensees must complete 12 hours of continuing education per renewal cycle. Of these, at least 3 hours must be an MREC-approved CORE course. The remaining 9 hours may be any MREC-approved core or elective courses.

questions.question_text: A broker completed 12 hours of elective CE but skipped the CORE requirement. Can the broker renew?
questions.correct_answer: No, 3 hours of CORE are mandatory
distractors: Yes, if the broker takes CORE next cycle
questions.explanation: Missouri requires 3 hours of CORE (mandatory) continuing education topics as part of the 12-hour total. Completing 12 hours of only elective courses does not satisfy the requirement. The licensee must complete 3 hours of the designated CORE topic to be eligible for renewal.

questions.question_text: What is the designated CORE continuing education topic for Missouri's 2026-2028 cycle?
questions.explanation: For the 2026-2028 renewal cycle, MREC has designated Fair Housing as the mandatory CORE continuing education topic. All licensees must complete 3 hours of approved Fair Housing CE during the cycle. The CORE topic changes each cycle and is selected by the MREC.

questions.question_text: When do Missouri broker licenses expire?
questions.explanation: Missouri broker licenses expire on June 30 of even-numbered years. Salesperson licenses expire on September 30 of even-numbered years. This staggered expiration schedule allows MREC to process renewals more efficiently across the two license types.

questions.question_text: A salesperson forgets to renew by September 30. She renews 2 months late. What is the total late penalty?
questions.explanation: Missouri imposes a delinquency fee of $50 per month or partial month, up to a $200 maximum; late fees are not prorated. At exactly 2 months late, the salesperson owes $100 in late penalties (2 x $50) in addition to the standard renewal fee.
